title: Anthropic Java SDK v0.5.0 — Production ready release
content: Anthropic Java SDK v0.5.0 reaches production stability with full streaming support, Spring Boot integration, virtual thread compatibility, and Maven Central publishing.

title: AI Coding and Small Steps — Kent Beck on incremental AI development
content: Kent Beck explores how the principle of small, safe steps applies to AI-assisted coding. Argues that AI tendency for large changes must be countered with disciplined TDD and commit discipline.

title: Observability for AI Systems — Charity Majors on AI debugging
content: Charity Majors outlines observability practices for AI-powered systems: tracing LLM calls, measuring hallucination rates, tracking prompt latency, and building dashboards for AI behavior.

title: AI Coding Workflow 2025 — Swyx evolved approach
content: Swyx shares an evolved AI coding workflow for 2025: when to use chat vs. inline assistance vs. autonomous agents, context hygiene practices, and output verification strategies.

title: AI Context Files Best Practices — What to put in CLAUDE.md
content: A practical guide to crafting effective AI context files for coding assistants. Covers what information to include, how to structure rules, and maintaining context files as living documents.

title: Code Ownership in the Age of AI — Team conventions
content: Engineering teams share conventions for maintaining code ownership when AI generates significant portions of the codebase: review requirements, attribution practices, and knowledge transfer.

title: AI Engineering Principles — Will Larson on AI-assisted development
content: Will Larson articulates engineering principles for AI-assisted development: maintaining intellectual engagement, building evaluation capacity, and preserving architectural judgment.

title: Specification-Driven AI Development — Writing AI-ready specs
content: How to write functional specifications that work well as AI coding prompts. Covers the level of detail needed, how to express constraints, and iterative spec refinement based on AI output.

title: AI Rubber Duck Debugging — Explaining code to Claude
content: A technique for debugging by explaining code to Claude without asking for a solution. The act of formulating the explanation often reveals the bug, and Claude questions guide the diagnosis.

title: AI-Assisted Component Design Patterns — Dan Abramov on React architecture
content: Dan Abramov shares patterns for using AI tools effectively in component design: when to generate vs. when to write by hand, and how to maintain design intentionality with AI assistance.

title: Effective AI Code Review Prompt Patterns — Nelson Elhage
content: Nelson Elhage catalogues prompt patterns for getting high-quality code review feedback from AI: structured review templates, security focus modes, and asking for specific code improvements.

title: AI Testing Practices — Property-based testing with AI assistance
content: Hillel Wayne approach to AI-assisted testing: using AI to generate property-based test cases, explore edge cases systematically, and maintain test suite coverage over time.

title: AI-Assisted Legacy Code Migration — Brownfield practices
content: Strategies for using AI coding tools to migrate legacy codebases: characterization test generation, incremental strangler fig pattern, and AI-assisted documentation of undocumented code.

title: AI-Assisted Architecture Reviews — The Architecture Elevator
content: Gregor Hohpe framework for using AI tools in architecture reviews: having Claude challenge assumptions, generate alternative designs, and identify architectural anti-patterns.

title: Staff Engineer AI Practices — Leveraging AI for technical leadership
content: How staff engineers use AI coding tools differently from individual contributors: for codebase archaeology, technical strategy documents, and mentoring through AI-assisted code examples.

title: AI Coding for Teams — O'Reilly practical guide
content: O'Reilly guide on integrating AI coding tools into engineering teams: establishing team conventions, handling disagreements about AI-generated code, and measuring productivity impact.

title: AI-Driven DevOps Practices — Automation and intelligence
content: DevOps teams share practices for integrating AI coding tools into CI/CD: automated code quality gates, AI-generated release notes, and intelligent deployment decision support.

title: The Science of AI Prompting 2024 — Research-backed techniques
content: Adrian Colyer summarizes research findings on effective AI prompting: why chain-of-thought works, optimal instruction placement, and evidence-based techniques for improving LLM accuracy.

title: Secure AI Coding Practices — OWASP AI security guide
content: OWASP-aligned guide to secure AI coding practices: preventing prompt injection in AI-assisted code, securing AI tool integrations, and safe handling of AI-generated credentials.

title: AI Code Quality at Scale — LinkedIn Engineering
content: LinkedIn Engineering shares practices for maintaining code quality at scale with AI assistance: custom linting rules for AI patterns, required human review stages, and code smell detection.

title: AI Infrastructure Patterns — Stripe Engineering
content: Stripe Engineering patterns for building AI-powered developer tools infrastructure: prompt version management, AB testing LLM outputs, and fallback strategies for model outages.

content: Complete API reference for all computer use action types: mouse_move, left_click, screenshot, key, type, and scroll with parameter definitions and example responses.

content: Protocol reference for the MCP ping/pong utility used to verify connection health between clients and servers with timing and timeout specifications.

content: Detailed documentation on implementing tools for Claude: defining tool schemas, handling tool calls, returning results, and managing multi-turn tool conversations.

content: Official Anthropic prompt library with categorized, tested prompts for common use cases: summarization, code review, data analysis, creative writing, and customer service.

content: Documentation covering Claude behavior in real-world deployments: how it handles ambiguity, honesty commitments, refusal behaviors, and guidance for content moderation.

content: Documentation for the MCP connection lifecycle: capability negotiation during initialization, the active request phase, and graceful shutdown procedures.

content: Documentation for Claude native PDF processing capability covering supported PDF types, page count limits, text extraction, and handling of embedded images.

content: API reference for Anthropic Admin API covering workspace management, user provisioning, API key administration, and organization settings for enterprise accounts.

content: Documentation listing all geographic regions where Anthropic API is available, regional endpoints, data residency information, and compliance certifications.

content: Anthropic best practices for designing effective tools for Claude: naming conventions, description quality, schema design, and avoiding common pitfalls in tool definitions.

content: Documentation for MCP elicitation, the mechanism that allows MCP servers to request additional information or confirmation from users through the client interface.

content: Documentation for implementing safety guardrails in Claude-powered applications: content filtering, output validation, rate limiting, and monitoring for misuse patterns.
